scenario.title: Interface directions
scenario.question: Which visual direction should the empty tag-filter result take?
inputs: STOP-01 resolution — unknown tags stay empty, no remote suggestions

options.label: Quiet text
options.claim: An empty result is a non-event — the calmest possible sentence and one exit, nothing else.

mockup.headline: No skills match “deploy”
mockup.body: Try another tag, or clear the filter to see all 5 skills.

pros: smallest possible surface; nothing to maintain
cons: gives no sense of what the catalog does contain
dead end unless the user notices the one action

options.label: Explain and suggest
options.claim: An empty result is a navigation moment — say why it is empty and show where the catalog actually is.

mockup.headline: Nothing is tagged “deploy” yet
mockup.body: The catalog covers 5 skills across these tags:

pros: turns the miss into a map of what exists
suggestion chips are real links into working filters
cons: more moving parts to keep in sync with real tags
chip row wraps awkwardly if the tag set grows

options.label: Catalog stays visible
options.claim: Never blank the canvas — keep the full inventory in sight, dimmed, and mark the miss inline.

mockup.notice: No skills match “deploy” — showing the full catalog, dimmed

pros: user never loses orientation or scroll position
the inventory itself advertises valid tags
cons: dimmed rows read as disabled controls to some users
notice + dimming is the heaviest treatment of the three
harder to keep accessible — dimmed text must stay legible

a.note: states the miss, not the shape of the catalog

c.note: the visible inventory is the answer

b.note: map of tags, but list is gone

c.note: server renders the same dimmed list
notice pushes the list below the fold

recommendation.reason: It is the only direction that converts the miss into forward motion: the tag chips are real filters, the copy is honest about why the result is empty, and the cost is bounded because the chip row derives from the same visibleSkills() data the catalog already renders.

unresolved: chip ordering (alphabetical vs frequency) once real usage exists
whether the clear-filter action reads better as a link than a button

accepted.acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — candidate pasted and confirmed after comparing all three rendered directions
accepted.recordTitle: Empty tag-filter result: Explain and suggest (direction B)
accepted.summary: With all three directions rendered against the identical five-skill dataset, direction B was accepted: it explains why the result is empty and turns the miss into navigation via real tag chips, at a bounded maintenance cost.

decisions.rationale: B is the only direction that gives the user somewhere to go; chips derive from visibleSkills() so they cannot drift from the real tag set.
decisions.acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — explicit confirmation of the pasted candidate

exampleWorking.rationale: B is the only direction that gives the user somewhere to go; chips derive from visibleSkills() so they cannot drift from the real tag set.

accepted.canonicalBody:
# Empty tag-filter result — accepted direction

Direction B (Explain and suggest) is accepted for the empty tag-filter state.
The empty state names the missing tag, states the catalog's real tag set as
chips that are live filters, and offers a clear-filter exit. Directions A
(Quiet text) and C (Catalog stays visible) are preserved with their change
conditions in the decision record.
